text.title: Pair with AI Agent Protocol Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Session shipped a commit but no acceptance test exists; the only proof the change worked is the agent's word.
antipattern.root-cause: Skipped intent-and-test-first because 'this one is small'.
antipattern.fix: Reject the PR; reopen the session with at least one named acceptance test.

antipattern.symptom: alembic upgrade head executed without human ratify; staging schema diverged from prod.
antipattern.root-cause: halt-before-side-effects not enforced in the agent's allowed-tools.
antipattern.fix: Add migration commands to the agent's disallowed list; require human confirm in the runner config.

antipattern.symptom: Session opened to 'add idempotency' but the diff also rewrites the refund state machine.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent went off-script; human did not close + reopen the session.
antipattern.fix: Revert the off-scope edits; open a separate session with the new explicit intent.

antipattern.symptom: PR body says 'fixed with Claude' but no transcript is attached; reviewer cannot audit how the diff was produced.
antipattern.fix: Block merge until the transcript link is in the PR body.
